nUr ifj’kn ¼u;s nUr dkWystks dh LFkkiuk] nUr dkWystksa esa v/;;u vFkok çf“k{k.k ds u;s vFkok mPPkÙkj ikB~;Øe [kksyuk vkSj nUr dkWystksa esa izos“k {kerk dks c<+kuk½ fofu;e] 2006Þ fofu;e 11 ds ckn ,d u;k fofu;e 11 , dks lfEefyr fd;k tk;sxkA ß11 , Lukrd iwoZ vkSj LukrdksÙkj Lrj esa LVkQ] Hkou] midj.k] LFkku] izf'k{k.k vkSj vU; lqfo/kkvksa ds lanHkZ esa ,sls ekU;rk izkIr laLFkku tks nUr fpfdRld vf/kfu;e 1948 vkSj blds vUrxZr cus fu;e ,oa fofu;e ftuesa dsUnz ljdkj dh flQkfj“kksa ij le;≤ ij fd, x;s la“kks/kuksa }kjk fu/kkZfjr vko“;drkvksa ls ifj’kn dks larq’V ugha dj ikrs gSa rks nUr fpfdRld vf/kfu;e dh /kkjk 16 d ds vUrxZr ,sls Lukrd iwoZ vkSj@vFkok LukrdksÙkj Lrj ds laLFkkuksa dh nUr ;ksX;rk dhs ekU;rk okil ys yh tk;sxh vkSj dsUnz ljdkj ,sls laLFkkuksa ds fo:)] ,sls ikB~;Øeksa ds v/;;u vkSj izf“k{k.k esa izos“k jksdus lfgr rF;ksa vkSj ifjfLFkfr;ksa ds eísutj tks mfpr le>sxh bu ij dkjZokbZ djsxhA ,sls nUr laLFkkuksa dks lquokbZ dk ,d volj fn;k tk;sxkAß MkW- lC;lkph lkgk] lfpo [विज्ञापन -III/4/असा./136/17] -- 51 of 57 -- 2 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART III—SEC. 4] ç/kku fofu;e] uker;k Hkkjrh; nUr ifj’kn ¼u;s nUr dkWystks dh LFkkiuk] nUr dkWystksa esa v/;;u vFkok çf“k{k.k ds u;s vFkok mPPkÙkj ikB~;Øe [kksyuk vkSj nUr dkWystksa esa izos“k {kerk dks c<+kuk½ fofu;e] 2006 fnukad 12 tuojh] 2006 dks Hkkjr ds jkti= ds Hkkx&III [k.M ¼4½ esa çdkf“kr ,oa vf/klwfpr fd;k x;k Fkk vkSj ftls ckn esa vf/klwpuk fnukad 16-01-2006] 21-02-2006] 03-011-2006] 22-05-2012] 11-03-2016 ds }kjk la“kksf/kr fd;k x;kA DENTAL COUNCIL OF INDIA NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 4th July, 2017 No. DE-22-2017.— In exercise of the powers conferred upon the Dental Council of India under Section 20 of the Dentists Act, 1948, the Dental Council of India, with the previous sanction of the Central Government, hereby makes the following Amendments to the existing Principal “Dental Council of India (Establishment of New Dental Colleges, Opening of New of Higher Course of Study or Training and Increase of Admission Capacity in Dental Colleges) Regulations, 2006” published and notified in Part III, Section 4 of the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, dated 12.01.2016:– 1. Short title and commencement:- (i) These Regulations may be called the “Dental Council of India (Establishment of New Dental Colleges, Opening of New of Higher Course of Study or Training and Increase of Admission Capacity in Dental Colleges) (9th Amendment) Regulations, 2017”. (ii) They shall come into force from the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the “Dental Council of India (Establishment of New Opening of New Dental College, Opening of New Higher Course of Study or Training and Increase of Admission Capacity in Dental Colleges) Regulations, 2006”, after Regulation 11, a new regulation 11 A shall be inserted:- Stop of admissions “11A When, upon report by the Executive Committee or the Visitor it appears to the Council that— Any recognized Dental institution does not satisfy the requirement of the Council at undergraduate and/or postgraduate level in terms of staff, building, equipment, accommodation, training and other facilities as prescribed in the Dentist’s Act, 1948 and Rules & Regulations made thereunder, as amended from time to time it may recommend to the Central Government for withdrawal of recognition of such dental qualification at undergraduate and/or postgraduate level under section 16 A of the Dentists Act and the Central Government may take appropriate action thereon as it may think fit in the facts and circumstances of each case including stoppage of admission at such course(s) of study and training, after an opportunity of hearing is granted to such dental institution”. Dr. SABYASACHI SAHA, Secy. [ADVT.-III/4/Exty./136/17] Foot Note: The Principal Regulations, namely, the Dental Council of India (Establishment of New Dental Colleges, Opening of New of Higher Course of Study or Training and Increase of Admission Capacity in Dental Colleges) Regulations, 2006, were published in Part III, Section, Section (4) of the Gazette of India vide Notification dated the 12th January, 2006 and amended vide notification dated 16.01.2006, 21.02.2006, 03.11.2006, 22.05.2012, 11.03.2016. vLirky dk LOkkeh gks vkSj mldk çca/ku djrk gks rks lac/kn~d fpfdRlk dkWyst ls 10 fd- eh- dh nwjh esa lM+d ekxZ }kjk 30 fdñehñ rd dh nwjh dks NwV çnku dh tk ldrh gSA MkWñ lC;lkph lkgk] lfpo [विज्ञापन -III/4/असा./135/17] ç/kku fofu;e] uke“;k] MhñlhñvkbZñ ¼u;s nUr dkWystks dh LFkkiuk] nUr dkWystksa esa v/;;u vFkok çf“k{k.k ds u;s vFkok mPPkÙkj ikB~;Øe [kksyuk vkSj nUr dkWystksa esa izos“k {kerk dks c<+kuk½ fofu;e] 2006 fnukad 12-01-2006 dh vf/klwpuk ds ek/;e ls Hkkjr ljdkj ds vlk/kkj.k jkti= ds Hkkx&III ds [kaM&4 esa çdkf“kr vkSj fnukad 16-01-2006] 21-02-2006] 02-02-2006] 03-11-2006] 22-05-2012] 11-03-2016 dh vf/klwpuk ds ek/;e ls la“kksf/kr fd;s x;s FksA DENTAL COUNCIL OF INDIA NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 28th June, 2017 No. DE-22-2017.— In exercise of the powers conferred upon the Dental Council of India under Section 20 of the Dentists Act, 1948, the Dental Council of India, with the previous sanction of the Central Government, hereby makes the following Amendment to the existing Principle “DCI (Establishment of New Opening of New Dental College, Opening of New or Higher Course of Study or Training and Increase of Admission Capacity in Dental Colleges) Regulations, 2006”, published in Part III, Section 4 of the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, dated 12.01.2006 namely:— 1. Short title and Commencement.— (i) These Regulations may be called the Draft “Dental Council of India (Establishment of New Opening of New Dental College, Opening of New or Higher Course of Study or Trainingand Increase of Admission Capacity in Dental Colleges) (11th Amendment) Regulations, 2017.” (ii) They shall come into force with effect from the date of their publication in theOfficial Gazette. 2. The following new proviso, after the existing proviso of clause (h) in sub-regulations (2) of Regulations 6 of the principal Regulations, 2006, be inserted as under:- “Provided that the distance of the attached medical college shall be relaxable only upto 30 kms, if the applicant owns and manages a General Hospital of not less than 100 beds with necessary infrastructure facilities including teaching pre-clinical, para-clinical and allied medical sciences, as per profile prescribed at annexure-I of the principal regulations and amended from time to time, in the campus of the proposed dental college. 3. The following proviso be substituted with the existing proviso of clause (hh) in sub-regulations (2) of Regulations 6 of the principle Regulations, 2006, as under:- “Provided that, no permission for starting of MDS Course in the speciality of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery shall be granted by the Central Government u/s 10A of Dentists (Amendment) Act, 1993 unless and until the desirous existing dental college is attached with a Government/Private Medical college approved/recognized by the Medical Council of India located at the distance of 10 kms. by road but the distance of 10 kms. at the attached medical college shall be relaxable up to 30 kms. by road, if the applicant owns and manages a General Hospital of not less than 100 beds with necessary infrastructure facilities including teaching pre-clinical, para-clinical and allied medical sciences, as per profile prescribed at annexure-I of the principal regulations and amended from time to time, in the campus of the proposed dental college.” DR. SABYASACHI SAHA, Secy. ljdkj vFkok la?k jkT; {ks= ç”kklu] tSlk Hkh ekeyk gks] çkIr dj fy;k gS( LukrdksÙkj ikB~;Øeksa esa ços”k {kerk c<+kus ds fy;s bldh vko”;drk ugha gSAß MkWñ lC;lkph lkgk] lfpo [foKkiu&III@4@vlk-@263@17] % ç/kku fofu;e] uke”;k] ÞMhñlhñvkbZñß ¼u;s nUr dkWystksa dh LFkkiuk] nUr dkWystksa esa v/;;u vFkok çf”k{k.k ds u;s vFkok mPPkÙkj ikB~;Øe [kksyuk vkSj nUr dkWystksa esa ços”k {kerk dks c<+kuk½ fofu;e] 2006ß fnukad 12-01-2006 dh vf/klwpuk ds ek/;e ls Hkkjr ljdkj ds vlk/kkj.k jkti= ds Hkkx&III ds [k.M&4 esa çdkf”kr vkSj fnukad 16-01-2006] 21-02-2006] 03-11-2006] 22-05-2012] 11-03-2016] 05-07-2017] 05-07-2017 dh vf/klwpuk ds ek/;e ls la”kksf/kr fd;s x;s FksA DENTAL COUNCIL OF INDIA NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 3rd October, 2017 No. DE-22(12)-2017.— In exercise of the powers conferred upon the Dental Council of India under Section 20 of the Dentists Act, 1948, the Dental Council of India, with the previous sanction of the Central Government, hereby makes the following Amendment to the existing Principle “DCI (Establishment of New Opening of New Dental College, Opening of New or Higher Course of Study or Training and Increase of Admission Capacity in Dental Colleges) Regulations, 2006”, published in Part III, Section 4 of the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, dated 12.01.2006 namely: — 1. Short title and Commencement: — (i) These Regulations may be called the “Dental Council of India (Establishment of New Opening of New Dental College, Opening of New or Higher Course of Study or Training and Increase of Admission Capacity in Dental Colleges) (12th Amendment) Regulations, 2017.” (ii) They shall come into force with effect from the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In “DCI (Establishment of New Opening of New Dental College, Opening of New or Higher Course of Study or Training and Increase of Admission Capacity in Dental Colleges) Regulations, 2006”, published in Part III, Section 4 of the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, dated 12.01.2006”, the following shall be deleted against point 13 (b) of the Qualifying Criteria as indicated therein:- “the certificate regarding desirability and feasibility for starting a new or higher course of study or training at the dental college has been obtained by the college from the State Government or the Union Territory administration, as the case may be;” 3. In the “Dental Council of India (Establishment of New Opening of New Dental College, Opening of New Higher Course of Study or Training and Increase of Admission Capacity in Dental Colleges) Regulations, 2006”, the existing proviso of regulation 19 clause (c) be substituted with the following proviso, as under: — “(c) the certificate regarding feasibility and desirability for increasing admission capacity at the dental college has been obtained by the applicant from the State Government or the Union territory administration, as the case may be; the same is not required for increasing admission capacity in post-graduate course/s” Dr. SABYASACHI SAHA, Secy. [ADVT. III/4/Exty./263/17] Foot Note: The Principle Regulation namely, the “DCI (Establishment of New Opening of New Dental College, Opening of New or Higher Course of Study or Training and Increase of Admission Capacity in Dental Colleges) Regulations, 2006” were published in Part III, Section, Section (4) of the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, on vide Notification dated the 12th January, 2006 and amended vide notification dated 16.01.2006, 21.02.2006, 03.11.2006, 22.05.2012, 11.03.2016, 05.07.2017, 05.07.2017.
